title: Iran oil revenue soars as others struggle due to Hormuz crisis; how much is it earning daily?
description: Iran is experiencing a dual benefit from rising oil prices, with daily revenue increasing significantly in March. Despite ongoing tensions, crude exports remain near pre-war levels, with activity at Kharg Island terminal picking up. Iran has also blocked ships to aggressor nations via the Strait of Hormuz.

‘Legally Immaterial’: Madhya Pradesh High Court rules husband can’t be booked for ‘unnatural’ sex with wife
Oil prices fall as Trump pauses attacks on Iranian energy plants, Brent at $105
description: Oil prices are soaring past $100 a barrel as the Middle East conflict nears a month, impacting global supply. Brent crude is trading above $107, while WTI is near $94.  Goldman Sachs predicts further increases before a gradual decline.  Iran's tightening control over the Strait of Hormuz and potential new fees on ships are key factors driving these price hikes.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Bitter battle for nine seats that can turn electoral fortunes
description: The LDF, the UDF and the NDA are locked in three-cornered fights in the constituencies of Nemom, Kazhakkoottam, Vattiyoorkkavu, Aranmula, Pala, Thrissur, Nattika, Palakkad and Manjeshwaram. The outcomes from these seats could even determine the final outcome of the polls.

title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz to April 6
description: Trump made the statement on March 26, in a social media post; he said he will hold off on bombing Iran's energy plants

description: The Navy said ‌the two boats left Isla Mujeres, in the Mexican Caribbean state ‌of Quintana Roo, on March 20 bound ‌for ⁠Havana with nine crew members ⁠of different nationalities on board

title: Seechewal raises issue of bank penalties on failing to keep minimum account balance
‘Only true religion’ claim an insult to others’ belief, says Allahabad high court
description: The Allahabad High Court ruled that claiming one religion is the 'only true religion' is wrong in secular India, as it disparages others and can attract Section 295A of the IPC. Justice Saurabh Srivastava dismissed a plea by a priest facing charges for allegedly stating Christianity was the sole true faith, emphasizing India's constitutional secularism.

Top executives of two biggest US companies are leaving and they say the reason is AI
description: Two prominent CEOs, from Coca-Cola and Walmart, have cited the rise of AI as a factor in their decisions to step down. Both leaders indicated that the evolving business landscape, driven by AI, necessitates new leadership for future growth phases. Their departures highlight a significant technological shift impacting executive decision-making at major corporations.

title: BJP shadow, Congress fate: Which way will the Muslim vote swing in Kerala?

title: His 3 children drowned in unfenced tank 18 years ago. Now, J-K man granted Rs 2 lakh each as compensation
NFL Trade rumor: Chargers linked to $90 m Dolphins star to boost Justin Herbert offense
description: Tyreek Hill trade talk picked up again as the Los Angeles Chargers are reportedly targeting Miami Dolphins playmaker Tyreek Hill, a $90M talent, to address their offensive struggles. A viral post suggested that Hill could join the Chargers or Chiefs after the NFL Draft. Money also plays a role. Hill may not command the full value after the injury. That makes this a smart gamble for Los Angeles. If the Chargers pull this off after the draft, it solves their biggest issue. It adds speed, forces defences to back off, and finally gives Herbert the weapon he has been missing.

Bin Laden and devils: TV show’s 22-year defamation war ends in apology before Calcutta High Court
‘They might die’: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi terrifies bowlers, called ‘Baby Hulk’
description: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, now 15, is making waves as a destructive hitter in Indian cricket and the IPL. His rapid rise includes an India A debut and a strong bond with captain Jitesh Sharma, who highlights his natural power and the fear he instills in bowlers. Sooryavanshi is poised for a key role with Rajasthan Royals in IPL 2026.

title: Rolling back rights: Karnataka’s gender minority communities question Trans Bill 2026
description: Karnataka has played a significant role in shaping India’s gender minorities rights movement, with activists, lawyers and groups laying the groundwork. Shilpa Elizabeth talks to the community that feels The Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill recently passed by  Parliament undoes some of the gains of the struggle

title: Murder caught on camera in Karnataka: Man slits wife’s throat, drives SUV over her body
description: A horrifying incident unfolded near Balurgi village where a man allegedly murdered his wife, Saheli, by slitting her throat and then running over her body with his car. The brutal act, witnessed by motorists who reportedly filmed it, has sparked outrage. Police are investigating the motive behind this shocking crime.

title: Magic Johnson opens up on living with HIV years after diagnosis
description: Magic Johnson recently revisited his journey living with HIV during a conference in Atlanta, offering a candid reflection shaped by decades of experience. From his shocking 1991 retirement to becoming a global advocate, Johnson turned fear into purpose. Supported by his wife Cookie and guided by medical progress, he continues to inspire through honesty, education, and resilience, proving his impact extends far beyond basketball.

title: U.S. judge blocks Pentagon's Anthropic blacklisting for now
description: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth’s unprecedented move, ​which followed Anthropic’s refusal to allow the ‌military to use AI chatbot Claude for U.S. surveillance or autonomous weapons, blocked Anthropic from certain military contracts.

Army puts to test its new ‘Shaurya’ drone squadron for tank units
description: The Indian Army is bolstering its armoured regiments with dedicated drone units, 'Shaurya Squadrons', to enhance surveillance, precision strikes, and electronic warfare.  Recently tested in a realistic battle scenario, these units aim to significantly boost battlefield awareness and response times for tank formations. This move reflects lessons learned from global drone-centric conflicts.

With his substantial contract, trade talks are intensifying. A potential deal with Miami could see Báez move for prospects, offering Detroit payroll relief and a clearer path for younger talent.

SC: Vague allegations no basis for dowry case
description: The Supreme Court has ruled that vague and unsupported allegations are insufficient to initiate criminal proceedings, quashing a dowry harassment case against an elderly couple. The bench emphasized that citizens must be vigilant about their rights and take timely action, especially in matrimonial disputes, as delays can cast aspersions on a case.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' grosses Rs 805 crore in India; worldwide total nears Rs 1,100 crore
description: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is a box office sensation, crossing Rs 800 crore gross in India within its first week. The film, directed by Adiya Dhar, has shown remarkable performance, earning an estimated Rs 674.17 crore net. Overseas collections also stand strong at Rs 261.92 crore, nearing its predecessor's international success.

title: SC: For requisitioned vehicles, govt to pay 3rd party damages
description: The Supreme Court ruled that if a private vehicle is requisitioned by a district administration and meets with an accident, the government, not the insurance company, is liable for compensation. The court stated that the owner is divested of custody and control during requisition, making the requisitioning authority responsible for incidents.

title: Too many meds in old age? Cutting back could be safe
description: Elderly individuals, especially those frail or with limited life expectancy, may no longer need all their long-term medications. A global review suggests carefully stopping some drugs can be safe, reducing risks like falls and confusion. Doctors emphasize a shift towards patient-centered care, where medication reduction, when supervised, can improve well-being and energy levels.

title: ‘Talks are ongoing’: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open strait of Hormuz by 10 days
Ex-rapper Balen Shah, 35, to take oath as Nepal PM today
description: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah, 35, is set to become Nepal's Prime Minister, a dramatic rise for the former Kathmandu mayor known for his clashes with the federal government. His confrontational style, including a threat to burn down Singha Durbar, propelled him to national fame.

title: Middle East crisis: India in Iran’s list of ‘friendly countries’ comes as a surprise
description: Iran has permitted India to use the Strait of Hormuz, allowing 20 Indian cargo vessels and 18 LPG tankers to pass. This decision, following diplomatic efforts by India, eases concerns about energy supply disruptions. Iran stated that ships linked to its adversaries will be denied passage through the strategic waterway.

title: Vice President to present 20th edition of Ramnath Goenka journalism awards today
Ashwin drops blunt verdict on MS Dhoni: ‘He shouldn’t play entire season if…’
description: Ravichandran Ashwin firmly believes MS Dhoni should start in Chennai Super Kings' playing XI if he's part of the squad, rejecting the Impact Player role. Ashwin emphasized Dhoni's unwavering self-belief and his crucial on-field guidance for new captain Ruturaj Gaikwad, highlighting his wicket-keeping and tactical acumen as vital contributions beyond just batting.

No shortage of oil and gas, won't impose curbs: Govt
description: The government has ruled out restrictions on petrol and diesel sales, assuring citizens of ample oil and gas availability and robust import arrangements.  Despite global fuel use curbs and misinformation-driven queues, India's petroleum and LPG supply remains secure and under control, with reserves and ongoing procurements ensuring uninterrupted dispensing at all retail outlets.

title: PM Modi to meet CMs today on West Asia conflict preparedness
description: Prime Minister Modi is set to convene a video conference with Chief Ministers, excluding those from poll-bound states, to assess national readiness for potential disruptions stemming from the West Asia conflict. The "Team India" spirit will guide discussions on energy supply concerns and preparedness, with the cabinet secretariat to engage excluded states separately.

title: As spotlight fades, Vadodara theatre searches for a second act
Dowry claims lives of thousands, do not give bail for such deaths mechanically: SC
description: The Supreme Court has cautioned high courts against granting bail in dowry death cases, deeming a Patna High Court order unsustainable. The bench emphasized the gravity of dowry deaths, a social evil causing thousands of women's unnatural deaths, and directed the accused husband to surrender.

title: No fuel shortage, insists Govt, warns action against spreading rumours
Fewer flights, no fare caps: Domestic airfares set to soar
description: Domestic air travel is poised for a significant price increase this summer, mirroring international flight costs. A 12% reduction in weekly domestic flights, coupled with the removal of fare caps and anticipated Aviation Turbine Fuel hikes, will drive up ticket prices. Only financially robust airlines are expected to maintain operations as demand and operating costs fluctuate.

title: Ballots without a country: a quiet case for pluralism in Myanmar
description: In a country where geography mirrors diversity and authority remains contested, elections in Myanmar unfold detached from realities on the ground; with key Opposition forces excluded and large swathes of the nation outside effective participation, the exercise underscores how the absence of pluralism and federal accommodation continue to undermine any meaningful democratic transition

title: Conduct of some damaging image of police force: Union minister Patil in Surat
President urged not to grant assent to transgender bill
description: Over 140 legal professionals and activists have urged President Murmu to reject the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026. They argue the bill, passed hastily, violates constitutional rights by removing the right to self-perceived gender identity and introducing a medical board for identity certificates, potentially excluding marginalized transgender individuals from essential protections.

title: India, China should strengthen communication and coordination, lead Global South toward greater development: Chinese envoy
Delhi leads state-wise donations to national parties, shows ADR analysis
description: Delhi leads national parties in donations for FY 2024-25, contributing Rs 2,639.4 crore. Maharashtra and Gujarat follow with significant contributions. The Association for Democratic Reforms report highlights a substantial increase in total donations, with corporate entities being the primary source.

title: Haryana’s cotton farms holding on by a thread
description: The Bt cotton revolution of the early 2000s had farmers in Haryana’s Hisar, Sirsa, Fatehabad, and Jind reaping rewards. Now, the pink bollworm is slashing yields and squeezing profits and the MSP is not enough. As farmers ditch cotton for paddy, the impact is soon likely to be felt by farm labourers, traders, and the economy as a whole, reports Ashok Kumar

title: India, China neighbours that can't be parted: Beijing envoy
description: China's envoy to India, Xu Feihong, asserted that India and China's progress stems from their people's efforts, not external aid, subtly countering US remarks. He urged win-win cooperation and cautioned against those seeking to sow discord between the neighbours. Xu also highlighted the need for China and India to champion developing nations amidst rising global challenges.

title: Paul Skenes pulled early after Pittsburgh Pirates opening day collapse
description: Paul Skenes had a shocking start to the 2026 MLB season, lasting just two outs and giving up five runs. Fielding errors and control issues made things worse, leading to an early exit. Manager Don Kelly said it was a pitch count decision. Despite strong preseason confidence, Skenes now faces an early setback but no injury concerns.

Eshwar Khandre says Karnataka govt. will recover money from IAF for giving 58 acres of forest land to BMRCL for compensation
description: Forest Minister B. Eshwar Khandre on Thursday told the Legislative Assembly that the Indian Air Force (IAF) authorities were not only in ‘illegal possession’ of forest land in Jarakbande Kaval village of Bengaluru North taluk as the land granted to them had been cancelled, but it had also given about 58 acres of forest land to BMRCL for monetary compensation

You've got mail: J&K's first postwoman clocks 25 letters a day, 30 years on foot
description: In Shopian's Hirapora village, 55-year-old Ulfat Bano has been Kashmir's first postwoman for over three decades, delivering mail on foot through challenging terrain and harsh winters. Despite the physical toll, her passion for connecting people fuels her dedication.  She finds immense satisfaction in bringing good news and parcels to her community, viewing her work as a vital service.

description: A professor at Sri Siddhartha Institute of Medical Sciences allegedly proposed to a student in class, sparking outrage and violence. Videos show the professor offering chocolates and professing love, which the student rejected. The situation escalated when students, including the girl, assaulted the professor with slippers after he left the classroom.

Modi alleged PNB colluded with CBI, leading to an FIR without his input, and ED's actions halted his operations.

The CAG report reveals Rs 52,525 crore spent on five guarantee schemes, consuming a significant portion of revenue and necessitating increased borrowing. This fiscal pressure impacts capital expenditure and raises concerns about future growth and debt servicing obligations.

What is ‘launching soon’? White House official page posts strange videos amid US‑Iran war

Despite millions of views, the White House offered no clarification, leaving the posts mysterious and widely debated.

Vertical peril: How faulty elevators are claiming lives in India’s high-rises
description: Recent lift accidents across India, including tragic fatalities in Kolkata and Hyderabad, highlight critical safety lapses. Mechanical failures, negligence in maintenance, and dangerous improvisations have led to devastating consequences. These incidents underscore the urgent need for rigorous inspections, professional repairs, and enhanced safety protocols to prevent further loss of life.

title: Iran's mines, mobile missiles, drones & geography: Why reopening Strait of Hormuz is no easy task
description: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil route, is currently blocked by Iran. This action follows a US-Israel offensive and the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran has created a tense maritime chokehold, impacting global oil prices. While diplomatic talks are ongoing, reopening the strait presents significant geographical and military challenges.

title: CEO of America's largest bank reveals one work-from-home trend he didn't notice earlier
description: JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon criticizes remote work, citing a lack of attentiveness in virtual meetings and its detrimental impact on young employees' learning and emotional intelligence. He argues that in-office presence is crucial for skill development and project ownership, contrasting it with the 'game playing' and reduced accountability he observes in remote setups.

title: Get rid of old assumptions: Microsoft HR makes it clear to employees that it's time to change
description: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman announced a significant HR restructuring, prioritizing adaptability and speed for the AI era. This shakeup includes leadership changes and new teams focused on accelerating HR operations, employee experience, and workforce skilling. The move signals a shift towards agility and a faster pace for the tech giant.

title: Ram Navami 2026: Date set for March 27; madhyahna muhurat 11:13 am–1:41 pm
description: Ram Navami, the birth anniversary of Lord Shri Ram, will be celebrated on March 26 and 27, 2026. The festival marks the end of Chaitra Navratri. Devotees will observe rituals and prayers. Special timings for Madhyahna Muhurat are highlighted for the celebrations. Ayodhya and Iskcon devotees will celebrate on March 27th.
